---
title: "Humanized mice EAE analysis Brain"
author: "Mattia Zaghi"
date: "2026-03-06"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

## What is this vignette for?
This Vignette contains all the necessary data analysis steps followed to analyzed the single cell RNA-seq of Mature humanized_mouse_braindendrocytes treated with FGF and Tomato controls including the code to generate the different figures.


##1.Load packages, configure directories and create the Seurat object

This section of the code is necessary to load all the necessary packages for the analysis, configure the directories and create the Seurat object that is going to be used for the rest of the analysis.

##1.1 Load packages, configure directories

1.First load all the necessary packages in order to run all the subsequent steps & set the working directory where all the output of this analysis will be saved and stored

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(Seurat)
library(future)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggthemes)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(openxlsx)
library(homologene)
library(openxlsx)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Mm.eg.db)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(purrr)
library(forcats)
library(readxl)
library(hdf5r)
library(patchwork)
library(pals)
require("knitr")


library(DoubletFinder)
opts_knit$set(root.dir = "/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/Humanized_mice_OD/")# change accordingly

#source("/home/mattia/FGF_signaling_induces_disease-associated_humanized_mouse_braindendrocytes/functions.R")
```

##1.2 Create the seurat object 

Let's start with loading the actual data. We will load separately all the brain samples samples from the cell ranger output and then everything will be merged into a unique object thet will be called brain hMS. 

1.load all the seurat object corrected by cell bender 
```{r}

# Base folder containing all samples
base_dir <- "/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/Humanized_mice_OD"

# Find all sample folders that contain "brain" in the name
brain_samples <- list.dirs(base_dir, recursive = FALSE, full.names = FALSE)
brain_samples <- brain_samples[grepl("brain", brain_samples)]

# Load each sample and create a Seurat object (with unique cell names)
seurat_list <- list()
for (sample in brain_samples) {
  h5_path <- file.path(base_dir, sample, "RNA_AAAAGGGG", "cellbender_output_seurat.h5")
  if (!file.exists(h5_path)) {
    warning("Missing file: ", h5_path)
    next
  }
  message("Loading ", sample)

  data <- Read10X_h5(filename = h5_path, use.names = TRUE)

  # Normalize feature names: collapse underscores and multiple dashes into a single dash
  fn <- rownames(data)
  fn <- gsub("_+", "-", fn)      # underscores -> dash
  fn <- gsub("-{2,}", "-", fn)   # collapse multiple dashes
  rownames(data) <- fn

  # Prefix cell barcodes with sample name so they are unique across samples
  colnames(data) <- paste0(sample, "_", colnames(data))

  seurat_obj <- CreateSeuratObject(
    counts = data,
    project = sample,
    min.cells = 3,
    min.features = 200
  )

  # Per-sample QC before merging
  seurat_obj[["percent.mt.mm10"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(
  seurat_obj,
  pattern = "mt-"
)

# human mito (hg38)
seurat_obj[["percent.mt.hg38"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(
  seurat_obj,
  pattern = "MT-"
)
seurat_obj[["percent.mt"]]<-seurat_obj[["percent.mt.mm10"]]+seurat_obj[["percent.mt.hg38"]]

  seurat_obj <- subset(seurat_obj, subset = percent.mt < 5)

  # Light preprocessing for doublet detection (on each sample independently using SCT)
  seurat_obj <- SCTransform(seurat_obj, verbose = FALSE)
  seurat_obj <- RunPCA(seurat_obj, verbose = FALSE)
  seurat_obj <- FindNeighbors(seurat_obj, dims = 1:10, verbose = FALSE)
  seurat_obj <- FindClusters(seurat_obj, resolution = 0.1, verbose = FALSE)
  seurat_obj <- RunUMAP(seurat_obj, dims = 1:10, verbose = FALSE)

  umap_before <- DimPlot(seurat_obj, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters") + ggtitle(paste(sample, "before doublet removal"))

  # Per-sample DoubletFinder (SCT input)
  nExp_poi <- round(0.075 * ncol(seurat_obj))
  nExp_poi.adj <- round(nExp_poi * 1.25)
  pK <- 0.09
  pN <- 0.25

  seurat_obj <- doubletFinder(seurat_obj, PCs = 1:10, pN = pN, pK = pK, nExp = nExp_poi, reuse.pANN = NULL, sct = TRUE)

  df_col <- grep("pANN_", colnames(seurat_obj@meta.data), value = TRUE)[1]
  if (is.na(df_col)) stop("DoubletFinder pANN metadata not found")

  seurat_obj <- doubletFinder(seurat_obj, PCs = 1:10, pN = pN, pK = pK, nExp = nExp_poi.adj, reuse.pANN = df_col, sct = TRUE)

  df_class_cols <- grep("DF.classifications_", colnames(seurat_obj@meta.data), value = TRUE)
  if (length(df_class_cols) == 0) stop("DoubletFinder classification column not found")

  # Use the most recent classification column (should be the second pass)
  df_class_col <- tail(df_class_cols, 1)
  message("Using doublet classification column: ", df_class_col)

  sel_singlets <- seurat_obj@meta.data[[df_class_col]] == "Singlet"
  if (sum(sel_singlets, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) {
    stop("No cells annotated as Singlet in ", df_class_col)
  }
  seurat_obj <- subset(seurat_obj, cells = rownames(seurat_obj@meta.data)[sel_singlets])

  # Recompute UMAP after doublet removal (SCT after singlet filtering)
  # Remove the old SCT assay 
  DefaultAssay(seurat_obj)<-"RNA"
  seurat_obj[["SCT"]] <- NULL
  seurat_obj <- SCTransform(seurat_obj, verbose = TRUE)
  seurat_obj <- RunPCA(seurat_obj, verbose = TRUE)
  seurat_obj <- FindNeighbors(seurat_obj, dims = 1:10, verbose = TRUE)
  seurat_obj <- FindClusters(seurat_obj, resolution = 0.1, verbose = TRUE)
  seurat_obj <- RunUMAP(seurat_obj, dims = 1:10, verbose = TRUE)

  umap_after <- DimPlot(seurat_obj, reduction = "umap", group.by = "seurat_clusters") + ggtitle(paste(sample, "after doublet removal"))

  # Print per-sample before/after UMAP side-by-side
  print(umap_before + umap_after)

  seurat_list[[sample]] <- seurat_obj
}
```



2.Now all the different samples are going to be merged in one unique object 

```{r}
seurat_list <- lapply(names(seurat_list), function(s) {
  obj <- seurat_list[[s]]
  obj$sample <- s
  obj
})
names(seurat_list) <- names(seurat_list)

humanized_mouse_brain <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y), seurat_list)

rm(seurat_list)
#remove metadata from previous single sample doublet removal analysis
humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["nCount_SCT"]]<-NULL
humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["nFeature_SCT"]]<-NULL
humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["SCT_snn_res.0.1"]]<-NULL
humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["seurat_clusters"]]<-NULL
obj@meta.data[["pANN_0.25_0.09_879"]]<-NULL
humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["pANN_0.25_0.09_867"]]<-NULL
```

filter away cells that have a very high mitocondrial content 

```{r}
humanized_mouse_brain <- subset(humanized_mouse_brain, subset = percent.mt < 5)
```


##2.Quality controls and cell selection

In this sections quality controls based selection of good will be performed on the merged object will be performed before proceeding with the rest of then analysis. 

1.Let's first plot some QCs metrics to have a look at the global dataset, this will give a general idea of the dataset quality

```{r}
# add the mitochondrial gene precentage 
# mouse mito (mm10)
humanized_mouse_brain[["percent.mt.mm10"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(
  humanized_mouse_brain,
  pattern = "mt-"
)

# human mito (hg38)
humanized_mouse_brain[["percent.mt.hg38"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(
  humanized_mouse_brain,
  pattern = "MT-"
)
humanized_mouse_brain[["percent.mt"]]<-humanized_mouse_brain[["percent.mt.mm10"]]+humanized_mouse_brain[["percent.mt.hg38"]]
```

check human vs mouse cells

```{r}
# Assume this is your merged object
obj <- humanized_mouse_brain
DefaultAssay(obj)<-"RNA"
obj<-JoinLayers(obj)
# Count UMIs per cell for each species (adjust prefixes as needed)
human_umis <- Matrix::colSums(
  GetAssayData(obj, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")[grep("^GRCh38-", rownames(obj)), , drop = FALSE]
)
mouse_umis <-Matrix::colSums(
  GetAssayData(obj, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")[grep("^mm10-", rownames(obj)), , drop = FALSE]
)

# Assign species label based on which count is higher
obj$species <- ifelse(human_umis > mouse_umis, "human", "mouse")

# Optionally mark ambiguous/mixed cells (e.g., 20–80% human)
ratio <- human_umis / (human_umis + mouse_umis)
obj$species[ratio > 0.2 & ratio < 0.8] <- "mixed"

# Write back
humanized_mouse_brain <- obj

#how many cells per sample
table(humanized_mouse_brain$orig.ident)

#how many cell per species
table(humanized_mouse_brain$species)

#how many cell per replicate
table(humanized_mouse_brain$sample)

```


```{r,fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
# Visualize QC metrics as a violin plot

QC_RNA <- data.frame(sample =  humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["sample"]],
                     Genes = humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["nFeature_RNA"]],
                     RNA = humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["nCount_RNA"]],
                     Mt = humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["percent.mt"]])
QC_RNA$sample <- as.factor(QC_RNA$sample)

# Function to add mean and median annotations for each sample above the violin plots
add_mean_median_annotation <- function(plot, data, x_var, y_var) {
  samples <- unique(data[[x_var]])
  for (sample in samples) {
    sample_data <- data[data[[x_var]] == sample, ]
    mean_value <- mean(sample_data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
    median_value <- median(sample_data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
    plot <- plot + 
      annotate("text", x = sample, y = max(sample_data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05, 
               label = paste("Mean:", round(mean_value, 2)), color = "blue", size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
      annotate("text", x = sample, y = max(sample_data[[y_var]], na.rm = TRUE) * 1.50, 
               label = paste("Median:", round(median_value, 2)), color = "red", size = 5, fontface = "bold")
  }
  return(plot)
}

# Plot for RNA
Transcripts <- ggplot(QC_RNA) +
  aes(x = sample, y = RNA, fill = sample, color = sample) +
  geom_violin(trim = TRUE, color = "#000000") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, color = "#000000", fill = "#ffffff", outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
"#1f77b4",  # blue
            "#ff7f0e",  # orange
            "#2ca02c",  # green
            "#d62728",  # red
            "#9467bd"
))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
"#1f77b4",  # blue
            "#ff7f0e",  # orange
            "#2ca02c",  # green
            "#d62728",  # red
            "#9467bd"
))+
  ggthemes::theme_base() +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Number of UMI per cell") +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect()) +
  theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 18, family = "Arial", angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18, family = "Arial"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, family = "Arial"),
        axis.line = element_line(size = 1))

Transcripts <- add_mean_median_annotation(Transcripts, QC_RNA, "sample", "RNA")

# Plot for Genes
Genes <- ggplot(QC_RNA) +
  aes(x = sample, y = Genes, fill = sample, color = sample) +
  geom_violin(trim = TRUE, color = "#000000") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, color = "#000000", fill = "#ffffff", outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
"#1f77b4",  # blue
            "#ff7f0e",  # orange
            "#2ca02c",  # green
            "#d62728",  # red
            "#9467bd"
))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
"#1f77b4",  # blue
            "#ff7f0e",  # orange
            "#2ca02c",  # green
            "#d62728",  # red
            "#9467bd"
))+
  ggthemes::theme_base() +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Number of Genes per cell") +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect()) +
  theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 18, family = "Arial", angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18, family = "Arial"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, family = "Arial"),
        axis.line = element_line(size = 1))

Genes <- add_mean_median_annotation(Genes, QC_RNA, "sample", "Genes")

# Plot for Mt
Mt <- ggplot(QC_RNA) +
  aes(x = sample, y =   Mt, fill = sample, color = sample) +
  geom_violin(trim = TRUE, color = "#000000") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, color = "#000000", fill = "#ffffff", outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
"#1f77b4",  # blue
            "#ff7f0e",  # orange
            "#2ca02c",  # green
            "#d62728",  # red
            "#9467bd"
))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
"#1f77b4",  # blue
            "#ff7f0e",  # orange
            "#2ca02c",  # green
            "#d62728",  # red
            "#9467bd"
))+
  ggthemes::theme_base() +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Percentage of Mt genes") +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect()) +
  theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 18, family = "Arial", angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18, family = "Arial"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, family = "Arial"),
        axis.line = element_line(size = 1))

Mt <- add_mean_median_annotation(Mt, QC_RNA, "sample", "Mt")

Transcripts
Genes
Mt

```

let's also visualize how is the relationship between counts, genes and mito content

```{r}

plot1 <- FeatureScatter(humanized_mouse_brain, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "percent.mt")
plot2 <- FeatureScatter(humanized_mouse_brain, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA")
plot1 + plot2
```







##3.Data integration, dimensionlity reduction and clustering

In this section we will perform the data integration, dimensionality reduction and clustering of the

##3.1 Dimensionality reduction and clustering without data integration
Despite in this experiment there are not multiple data batches and replicate we will first perform a clustering with data ingtegration and without and look at the difference

1.Data normalization, variable features finding PCA

```{r}
options(future.globals.maxSize = 100e+09)

humanized_mouse_brain[["SCT"]]<-NULL

# Log-normalized analysis on RNA
humanized_mouse_brain <- NormalizeData(humanized_mouse_brain, assay = "RNA")
humanized_mouse_brain <- FindVariableFeatures(humanized_mouse_brain, assay = "RNA")
humanized_mouse_brain <- ScaleData(humanized_mouse_brain, assay = "RNA")
humanized_mouse_brain <- RunPCA(humanized_mouse_brain, assay = "RNA", reduction.name = "pca_lognorm")

# SCT analysis on SCT assay
humanized_mouse_brain <- SCTransform(humanized_mouse_brain, verbose = TRUE)
humanized_mouse_brain <- FindVariableFeatures(humanized_mouse_brain, assay = "SCT")
humanized_mouse_brain <- RunPCA(humanized_mouse_brain, assay = "SCT", reduction.name = "pca_SCT")
```

2.Having a look at the elbow plot to understand how many PCs are really informative, this value will be used in the subsequent analysis steps 
```{r}
p1<-ElbowPlot(humanized_mouse_brain,reduction = "pca_SCT")
p1
```
After 15 SD the elbow plot becomes flat indicating that the majority of the information is explained by the first 15 dimension, this value will be used for the rest of the analysis.

3.Clustering without data integration

```{r}
humanized_mouse_brain <- FindNeighbors(humanized_mouse_brain, dims = 1:15, reduction = "pca_SCT")
humanized_mouse_brain <- FindClusters(humanized_mouse_brain, resolution = 0.1, cluster.name = "unintegrated_clusters")
```
4. Plot UMAP with samples and clustering
```{r,fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
humanized_mouse_brain <- RunUMAP(humanized_mouse_brain, dims = 1:15, reduction = "pca_SCT", reduction.name = "umap.unintegrated")
# visualize by batch and cell type annotation

my_colors <- c(
  "#1F77B4", "#FF7F0E", "#2CA02C", "#D62728", "#9467BD",
  "#8C564B", "#17BECF", "#BCBD22", "#7F7F7F", "#e377c2",
  "#aec7e8", "#ffbb78", "#98df8a"
)


p1<-DimPlot(humanized_mouse_brain, reduction = "umap.unintegrated", group.by = c("unintegrated_clusters"),cols = my_colors)+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p2<-DimPlot(humanized_mouse_brain, reduction = "umap.unintegrated", group.by = c("orig.ident"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p3<-DimPlot(humanized_mouse_brain, reduction = "umap.unintegrated", group.by = c("sample"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )


p1 
p2
p3
saveRDS(humanized_mouse_brain,"/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/Humanized_mice_OD/humanized_mouse_brain_doublets.rds")
```

```{r,fig.height=10, fig.width=20}
colors_vector <- c(
  "CFA_brain_1" = "#E41A1C", 
  "CFA_brain_2" = "#377EB8", 
  "EAE_brain_1" = "#984EA3", 
  "EAE_brain_2" = "#FF7F00", 
  "EAE_brain_3" = "#FFFF33"
)

# Create the contingency table
t <- table(Cluster=humanized_mouse_brain$unintegrated_clusters, Batch=humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["sample"]])
t <- t[, rev(names(colors_vector))]

# Convert the table to a data frame
df <- as.data.frame(t)

# Calculate the percentage of each plate within each cluster
df <- df %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%mutate(Percent = Freq / sum(Freq) * 100)

# Plot the data
ggplot(df, aes(x = Cluster, y = Percent, fill = Batch)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = rev(colors_vector)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 18),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18,family = "Arial")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Percentage of cells", x = "Cluster", fill = "Animal") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 3, title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5))
```







##3.2 Dimensionality reduction and clustering with data integration
In this section the dimensionality reduction will be performed including data integration and SCT transformation which should make the dataset more homogeneus and prevent any differences that are not due to biological variation.

1. SCT transformation, PCA running, clustering and dimensionality reduction again using 15 PCs as shown by the elbowplot above

```{r}

humanized_mouse_brain <- IntegrateLayers(
  object = humanized_mouse_brain,
  method = HarmonyIntegration,
  orig.reduction = "pca_SCT", new.reduction = "harmony",
  normalization.method = "SCT",
  verbose = F
)
humanized_mouse_brain <- FindNeighbors(humanized_mouse_brain, dims = 1:15, reduction = "harmony")
humanized_mouse_brain <- FindClusters(humanized_mouse_brain, resolution = 0.1,cluster.name = "harmony_clusters")
```

2. Perform Dimensionality reduction
```{r}
humanized_mouse_brain <- RunUMAP(humanized_mouse_brain, dims = 1:15, reduction = "harmony",reduction.name = "umap.harmony")
```

3.Plot UMAP with clusters and sample to check that the data integration effectively results in a better homogeneus dataset 

```{r}


pal13 <- pals::glasbey(13)         # 13 very distinct colors
pal13

p1<-DimPlot(humanized_mouse_brain, reduction = "umap.harmony", group.by = c("harmony_clusters"), cols = pal13)+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+theme_minimal()+
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
     plot.title = element_blank()

  )


p2<-DimPlot(humanized_mouse_brain, reduction = "umap.harmony", group.by = c("sample"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+
  ggtitle("") +  # Add your title here
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

col<- c(
  mouse = "grey80",
  human = "firebrick3"
)


p3<-DimPlot(humanized_mouse_brain, reduction = "umap.SCT", group.by = c("species"), cols = col)+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+
  ggtitle("") +  # Add your title here
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p1
p2
p3
```


clusters proportions

```{r}
colors_vector <- c(
  "CFA_brain_1" = "#E41A1C", 
  "CFA_brain_2" = "#377EB8", 
  "EAE_brain_1" = "#984EA3", 
  "EAE_brain_2" = "#FF7F00", 
  "EAE_brain_3" = "#FFFF33"
)

# Create the contingency table
t <- table(Cluster=humanized_mouse_brain$harmony_clusters, Batch=humanized_mouse_brain@meta.data[["sample"]])
t <- t[, rev(names(colors_vector))]

# Convert the table to a data frame
df <- as.data.frame(t)

# Calculate the percentage of each plate within each cluster
df <- df %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%mutate(Percent = Freq / sum(Freq) * 100)

# Plot the data
ggplot(df, aes(x = Cluster, y = Percent, fill = Batch)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = rev(colors_vector)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 18),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18,family = "Arial")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Percentage of cells", x = "Cluster", fill = "Animal") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 3, title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5))
```


7.Compute and save cluster markers of the different population in the MOL subclustering. This will be one of the supplementary tables to be attached to be included. 

```{r}

# find markers for every cluster compared to all remaining cells, report only the positive
# ones

DefaultAssay(humanized_mouse_brain)<-"SCT"
humanized_mouse_brain <- PrepSCTFindMarkers(humanized_mouse_brain, assay = "SCT",verbose = T)
humanized_mouse_brain_markers <- FindAllMarkers(humanized_mouse_brain, only.pos = TRUE)


 # Create a new workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# Loop through each cluster and add data to a new sheet
for (cluster_value in unique(humanized_mouse_brain_markers$cluster)) {
  cluster_data <- subset(humanized_mouse_brain_markers, cluster == cluster_value)
  sheet_name <- paste0("Cluster_", cluster_value)
  addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
  writeData(wb, sheet_name, cluster_data)
}

# Save the workbook to a file
saveWorkbook(wb, "/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/Humanized_mice_OD/Seurat_clusters_broad_brain.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```

4.Now let's plot some markers genes to rename and homogenize clusters. After that the final steps of the analysis will be performed.The code use to generate the plots is used in the Supplementary Fig.S3C.


```{r,fig.height=80, fig.width=80}
markers <- c(
  # Cluster 0 - Cerebellar Granule Cells
  "mm10-Gabra6", "mm10-Cbln1", "mm10-Cbln3", "mm10-Neurod1", "mm10-Barhl2",
  # Cluster 1 - Excitatory Cortical Neurons (Glutamatergic)
  "mm10-Tbr1", "mm10-Satb2", "mm10-Emx1", "mm10-Neurod6",
  # Cluster 2 - GABAergic Interneurons (SST/VIP subtypes)
  "mm10-Lhx6", "mm10-Lhx8", "mm10-Sst", "mm10-Vip", "mm10-Tac2", "mm10-Slc5a7", "mm10-Nxph2",
  # Cluster 3 - GABAergic Inhibitory Neurons (Striatal/MGE-derived)
  "mm10-Dlx1", "mm10-Dlx2", "mm10-Gad1", "mm10-Sp9", "mm10-Meis2", "mm10-Pbx3",
  # Cluster 4 - Mature Oligodendrocytes
  "mm10-Plp1", "mm10-Mbp", "mm10-Apod", "mm10-Ptgds",
  # Cluster 5 - Astrocytes
  "mm10-Atp1a2", "mm10-Slc1a3", "mm10-Apoe", "mm10-Gja1", "mm10-Gli1", "mm10-Mfge8",
  # Cluster 6 - Medium Spiny Neurons (Striatal)
  "mm10-Drd1", "mm10-Drd2", "mm10-Adora2a", "mm10-Gpr88", "mm10-Rgs9", "mm10-Rarb",
  # Cluster 7 - Myelinating Oligodendrocytes
  "mm10-Klk6", "mm10-Gjc2", "mm10-Ugt8a", "mm10-Gjb1",
  # Cluster 8 - Cerebellar Inhibitory Interneurons (MLI/Pvalb+)
  "mm10-Pvalb", "mm10-Pax2", "mm10-Kit", "mm10-Tfap2a", "mm10-Tfap2b", "mm10-Skor1",
  # Cluster 9 - Microglia
  "mm10-Tmem119", "mm10-Hexb", "mm10-Trem2", "mm10-Tyrobp", "mm10-Inpp5d", "mm10-Itgam", "mm10-Cd14", "mm10-Adgre1",
  # Cluster 10 - Thalamic Neurons
  "mm10-Synpo2", "mm10-Zfhx3", "mm10-Shox2", "mm10-Lef1", "mm10-Slitrk6",
  # Cluster 11 - Oligodendrocyte Precursor Cells (OPCs)
  "mm10-Pdgfra", "mm10-Cspg4", "mm10-Vcan",
  # Cluster 12 - Purkinje Cells (Cerebellar)
  "mm10-Car8", "mm10-Pcp2", "mm10-Ppp1r17", "mm10-Col18a1", "mm10-Atp2a3"
)

# 1) create a list of plots (one per gene)
plots <- FeaturePlot(
  humanized_mouse_brain,
  features = markers,
  reduction = "umap.unintegrated",
  cols = c("lightgrey", "magenta"),
  combine = FALSE
)

# 2) apply consistent theme / title formatting to every plot
plots <- lapply(plots, function(p) {
  p +
    labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 64, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 50),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 50),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 50),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 52),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_blank()
    )
})

# 3) combine into a grid (e.g., 2 columns)
p <- wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 4)
p
```


5.Cluster renaming, based on the very broad classification OL, OPCs or microglia, this clustering to subset only MOLs to subsequently use them in the next analysis. The UMAP plot with the samples correponds to fig.3C and the UMAP plot with the broad cell types corresponds to Fig.S3B. 

```{r,fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
broad_clustering <- c("Cerebellar_Granule_Cells","Excitatory_Cortical_Neu","SST_Gaba_Neu","Stria_MGE_Inter_Neu","MOL",
                      "Astrocytes","Medium_Spiny_Neu_Stria","Myelin_MOL","Cerebellar_Inhib_Interneu_Pvalb+",
                      "Microglia","Thal_Neu","OPcs","Purkinje_Cells")

names(broad_clustering) <- levels(humanized_mouse_brain)
humanized_mouse_brain <- RenameIdents(humanized_mouse_brain, broad_clustering)
humanized_mouse_brain$broad_clustering <- Idents(humanized_mouse_brain) #create a new metadata colums with the broad clustering

# Check the new identities
table(Idents(humanized_mouse_brain))

my_colors <- c(
  "#1F77B4", "#FF7F0E", "#2CA02C", "#D62728", "#9467BD",
  "#8C564B", "#17BECF", "#BCBD22", "#7F7F7F", "#e377c2",
  "#aec7e8", "#ffbb78", "#98df8a"
)

p1<-DimPlot(humanized_mouse_brain, reduction = "umap.rpca_SCT",group.by = "orig.ident")+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

colors_3 <- c("red", "#56B4E9", "#009E73")  # select the colors you want for the UMAP

p2<-DimPlot(humanized_mouse_brain, reduction = "umap.unintegrated",group.by = "broad_clustering", cols = my_colors)+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )


p2
```

Dotplot for the broad clustering 


```{r,fig.height=20, fig.width=27}

markers <- c(
  # Cluster 0 - Cerebellar Granule Cells
  "mm10-Gabra6", "mm10-Cbln1", "mm10-Cbln3", "mm10-Neurod1", "mm10-Barhl2",
  # Cluster 1 - Excitatory Cortical Neurons (Glutamatergic)
  "mm10-Tbr1", "mm10-Satb2", "mm10-Emx1", "mm10-Neurod6",
  # Cluster 2 - GABAergic Interneurons (SST/VIP subtypes)
  "mm10-Lhx6", "mm10-Lhx8", "mm10-Sst", "mm10-Vip", "mm10-Tac2", "mm10-Slc5a7", "mm10-Nxph2",
  # Cluster 3 - GABAergic Inhibitory Neurons (Striatal/MGE-derived)
  "mm10-Dlx1", "mm10-Dlx2", "mm10-Gad1", "mm10-Sp9", "mm10-Meis2", "mm10-Pbx3",
  # Cluster 4 - Mature Oligodendrocytes
  "mm10-Plp1", "mm10-Mbp", "mm10-Apod", "mm10-Ptgds",
  # Cluster 5 - Astrocytes
  "mm10-Atp1a2", "mm10-Slc1a3", "mm10-Apoe", "mm10-Gja1", "mm10-Gli1", "mm10-Mfge8",
  # Cluster 6 - Medium Spiny Neurons (Striatal)
  "mm10-Drd1", "mm10-Drd2", "mm10-Adora2a", "mm10-Gpr88", "mm10-Rgs9", "mm10-Rarb",
  # Cluster 7 - Myelinating Oligodendrocytes
  "mm10-Klk6", "mm10-Gjc2", "mm10-Ugt8a", "mm10-Gjb1",
  # Cluster 8 - Cerebellar Inhibitory Interneurons (MLI/Pvalb+)
  "mm10-Pvalb", "mm10-Pax2", "mm10-Kit", "mm10-Tfap2a", "mm10-Tfap2b", "mm10-Skor1",
  # Cluster 9 - Microglia
  "mm10-Tmem119", "mm10-Hexb", "mm10-Trem2", "mm10-Tyrobp", "mm10-Inpp5d", "mm10-Itgam", "mm10-Cd14", "mm10-Adgre1",
  # Cluster 10 - Thalamic Neurons
  "mm10-Synpo2", "mm10-Zfhx3", "mm10-Shox2", "mm10-Lef1", "mm10-Slitrk6",
  # Cluster 11 - Oligodendrocyte Precursor Cells (OPCs)
  "mm10-Pdgfra", "mm10-Cspg4", "mm10-Vcan",
  # Cluster 12 - Purkinje Cells (Cerebellar)
  "mm10-Car8", "mm10-Pcp2", "mm10-Ppp1r17", "mm10-Col18a1", "mm10-Atp2a3"
)

p1<-DotPlot(humanized_mouse_brain, features = markers,group.by = "broad_clustering",cols = c("lightgrey", "firebrick"))+
  xlab("")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = markers) + 
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 24,angle = 90),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size =24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24,family = "Arial")
  ) 
p1
```





##4.MOL subsetting, and reclustering of MOLs only 

##4.1 Select only MOLs from the whole dataset

With this line of code we will create a new Seurat object that will contain only MOLs that we will use for the subsequents analysis steps

```{r}



OL <- subset(humanized_mouse_brain, idents = c("Myelin_MOL","MOL","OPcs"))            


```

##4.2 Reclustering of MOLs

1.Renormalize the datasets and recluster using only MOL cells   

```{r}
DefaultAssay(OL)<-"RNA"

# Remove the old SCT assay 
OL[["SCT"]] <- NULL

# Now run SCTransform renormalizing with only the OL_OPC cells 
OL <- SCTransform(OL, vst.flavor = "v2", verbose = FALSE)

# Continue with custom-named reductions
OL <- RunPCA(OL, verbose = T, reduction.name = "pca_OL_SCT")


OL<- FindNeighbors(OL, reduction = "pca_OL_SCT", dims = 1:15, 
                      graph.name = c("OL_nn", "OL_snn"))

OL <- FindClusters(OL, resolution = 0.1, 
                     graph.name = "OL_snn",
                     cluster.name = "OL_clusters")

OL <- RunUMAP(OL, reduction = "pca_OL_SCT", dims = 1:15, 
                reduction.name = "umap_OL")


```

2.Plot UMAP with samples and the New MOLs clusters

```{r}

my_colors <- c(
  "#1F77B4",
  "#FF7F0E",
  "#2CA02C",
  "#D62728",
  "#9467BD",
  "#8C564B",
  "#17BECF"
)

p1<-DimPlot(OL, reduction = "umap_OL", group.by = c("OL_clusters"), cols=my_colors)+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with new clustering
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p2<-DimPlot(OL, reduction = "umap_OL", group.by = c("orig.ident"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p3<-DimPlot(OL, reduction = "umap_OL", group.by = c("sample"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p4<-DimPlot(OL, reduction = "umap_OL", group.by = c("broad_clustering"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p1
p2
p3
p4
```







clusters proportions

```{r}
colors_vector <- c(
  "CFA_brain_1" = "#E41A1C", 
  "CFA_brain_2" = "#377EB8", 
  "EAE_brain_1" = "#984EA3", 
  "EAE_brain_2" = "#FF7F00", 
  "EAE_brain_3" = "#FFFF33"
)

# Create the contingency table
t <- table(Cluster=OL$OL_clusters, Batch=OL@meta.data[["sample"]])
t <- t[, rev(names(colors_vector))]

# Convert the table to a data frame
df <- as.data.frame(t)

# Calculate the percentage of each plate within each cluster
df <- df %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%mutate(Percent = Freq / sum(Freq) * 100)

# Plot the data
ggplot(df, aes(x = Cluster, y = Percent, fill = Batch)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = rev(colors_vector)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 18),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18,family = "Arial")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Percentage of cells", x = "Cluster", fill = "Animal") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 3, title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5))
```

3.Plotting marker genes to review the clustering and rename the different populations of MOLs

```{r,fig.height=20, fig.width=20}

markers <- c(
  #MOL5/6–EAE-b
  "mm10-Serpina3n","mm10-Nlrc5",
  #MOL5/6–EAE-a
  "mm10-Plin4","mm10-Sult1a1","mm10-Zfand4",
  #MOL1/2 EAE
  "mm10-Klk8","mm10-Itga8", "mm10-Tlr3",
  #MHC-II
  "mm10-H2-Aa", "mm10-H2-Ab1", "mm10-H2-Eb1", "mm10-Cd74", "mm10-Ctss",
  #interferon
  "mm10-Ifih1", "mm10-Irf7", "mm10-Irgm1", "mm10-Irgm2", "mm10-Igtp","mm10-Zbp1",
   #cycling_DAO
  "mm10-Cdkn1a","mm10-Ccnd1","mm10-Nav3","mm10-Tmsb10","mm10-Lrrfip1",
  # OPC1
  "mm10-Rph3a", "mm10-Adora2b","mm10-Pdgfra","mm10-Cspg4",
  # OPC2
  "mm10-Phyhd1","mm10-Fcgr2b","mm10-Mylk",
  # OPC3
  "mm10-Slc14a1","mm10-Lgals1","mm10-Myrf",
  #MOL 5/6
  "mm10-Ptgds", "mm10-Opalin", "mm10-Qdpr", "mm10-Il33", "mm10-Apoe", "mm10-Cd63", "mm10-Jph4","mm10-Fgfr2"
  
)

p1<-DotPlot(OL, features = markers,group.by = "OL_clusters",cols = c("lightgrey", "firebrick"))+
  xlab("")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = markers) + 
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 24,angle = 90),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size =24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24,family = "Arial")
  ) 
p1
```


```{r,fig.height=20, fig.width=20}

markers <- c(
  #MOL1
  "mm10-Fosb","mm10-Dusp1","mm10-Dnajb1","mm10-Btg2","mm10-Hspa1a","mm10-Hspa1b","mm10-Tob1","mm10-Mal",
  #MOL2
  "mm10-Anxa5","mm10-Klk6","mm10-Mgst3","mm10-S100b","mm10-Tppp3","mm10-Cyp51","mm10-Plekhb1",
  # MFOL 1/2
  "mm10-Ctps","mm10-Tmem141","mm10-Opalin","mm10-Ttyh1", "mm10-Plekha1", "mm10-Ddr1", "mm10-Cd9", "mm10-Sirt2","mm10-Arpc1b",
  # NFOL1/2
  "mm10-Sema6a","mm10-Mag","mm10-Mobp","mm10-Mbp","mm10-Rras2","mm10-Prom1"

  
)

p1<-DotPlot(OL, features = markers,group.by = "OL_clusters",cols = c("lightgrey", "firebrick"))+
  xlab("")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = markers) + 
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 24,angle = 90),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size =24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24,family = "Arial")
  ) 
p1
```

Marker features calculations in OLs

```{r}

# find markers for every cluster compared to all remaining cells, report only the positive
# ones

DefaultAssay(OL)<-"SCT"
OL<- PrepSCTFindMarkers(OL, assay = "SCT",verbose = T)
OL_markers<- FindAllMarkers(OL, only.pos = TRUE)


 # Create a new workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# Loop through each cluster and add data to a new sheet
for (cluster_value in unique(OL_markers$cluster)) {
  cluster_data <- subset(OL_markers, cluster == cluster_value)
  sheet_name <- paste0("Cluster_", cluster_value)
  addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
  writeData(wb, sheet_name, cluster_data)
}

# Save the workbook to a file
saveWorkbook(wb, "/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/Humanized_mice_OD/Seurat_clusters_OLs.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```

Plotting cluster Markers



```{r,fig.height=20, fig.width=30}



markers <- c(
  # Cluster 0 - Myelinating Oligodendrocytes (MOL)
  "mm10-Ugt8a","mm10-Car2","mm10-Cdh20","mm10-Rtn4","mm10-Rnf220","mm10-Sox2ot","mm10-St18","mm10-Ptprd","mm10-Plp1","mm10-Nkain2",
  # Cluster 1 - Mature Oligodendrocytes (MOL) - homeostatic/metabolic
  "mm10-Garnl3","mm10-Mt1","mm10-Zbtb16","mm10-Polr3e","mm10-Slc6a1","mm10-Slc25a13","mm10-Otud7a","mm10-Mast3","mm10-Elmo1","mm10-Plcl1",
  # Cluster 2 - Mature Oligodendrocytes (MOL) - Apod/Ptgds-high
  "mm10-Apod","mm10-Ptgds","mm10-Phyhd1","mm10-Rsrp1","mm10-Edil3","mm10-Acaca","mm10-Neat1","mm10-St6galnac3","mm10-Anln","mm10-Hif3a",
  # Cluster 3 - Neuronal contamination (Cerebellar Granule Cells)
  "mm10-Kcnip4","mm10-Meg3","mm10-Chn2","mm10-Car10","mm10-Fgf14","mm10-Kcnd2","mm10-Cntnap2","mm10-Rbfox1","mm10-Negr1","mm10-Nrxn1",
  # Cluster 4 - OPCs / Committed OL Precursors (COP)
  "mm10-Lhfpl3","mm10-Xylt1","mm10-Tnr","mm10-Sgcd","mm10-Dscam","mm10-Adarb2","mm10-Ptprz1","mm10-Lrrtm4","mm10-Csmd1","mm10-Kcnip1",
  # Cluster 5 - OPCs (early/proliferative)
  "mm10-Nxph1","mm10-Gpc5","mm10-Sox5","mm10-Sox6","mm10-Slc35f1","mm10-Pcdh7","mm10-Ptprt","mm10-Luzp2","mm10-Nav3","mm10-Pdgfra",
  # Cluster 6 - Neuronal contamination (GABAergic/Glutamatergic)
  "mm10-Grin2b","mm10-Kcnb2","mm10-Atp1b1","mm10-Dclk1","mm10-Tenm3","mm10-Epha6","mm10-Kalrn","mm10-Gria1","mm10-Dlgap2","mm10-Etl4"
)

  


p1<-DotPlot(OL, features = markers,group.by = "OL_clusters",cols = c("lightgrey", "firebrick"))+
  xlab("")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = markers) + 
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 24,angle = 90),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size =24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24,family = "Arial")
  ) 
p1
```









1.Compute the GO terms significantly enriched

```{r}
# marker genes computed above

pos_markers <- OL_markers%>%
  dplyr::select(cluster, gene)

# convert symbols to ENTREZID per cluster
marker_list <- split(pos_markers$gene, pos_markers$cluster)

# convert once (union) and map back
all_genes <- unique(unlist(marker_list))

all_genes_clean <- sub("^mm10-", "", all_genes)

gene_map <- bitr(all_genes_clean, fromType = "SYMBOL", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db)
sym2ent <- setNames(gene_map$ENTREZID, gene_map$SYMBOL)

marker_list_clean <- lapply(marker_list, function(x) sub("^mm10-", "", x))

entrez_by_cluster <- lapply(marker_list_clean, function(x) na.omit(unname(sym2ent[x])))

ego_list <- lapply(names(entrez_by_cluster), function(cl){
  enrichGO(gene          = entrez_by_cluster[[cl]],
           OrgDb         = org.Mm.eg.db,
           keyType       = "ENTREZID",
           ont           = "BP",
           pAdjustMethod = "BH",
           pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
           qvalueCutoff  = 0.2,
           readable      = TRUE)
}) #list of enriched terms per cluster
names(ego_list) <- names(entrez_by_cluster)

```


2.Create an Excel file to save the results, to be attached as supplementary material

```{r}

# Create a new workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# Loop through each cluster and add enrichment results as a sheet
for (cluster_name in names(ego_list)) {
  
  # Extract results from enrichResult object
  if (!is.null(ego_list[[cluster_name]]) && nrow(ego_list[[cluster_name]]@result) > 0) {
    
    # Get the results as a data frame
    go_results <- as.data.frame(ego_list[[cluster_name]])
    
    # Create sheet name (Excel has 31 char limit for sheet names)
    sheet_name <- substr(cluster_name, 1, 31)
    
    # Add worksheet
    addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
    
    # Write data
    writeData(wb, sheet_name, go_results)
    
    cat(sprintf("Added sheet for cluster: %s (%d GO terms)\n", 
                cluster_name, nrow(go_results)))
    
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("No significant GO terms for cluster: %s\n", cluster_name))
  }
}

# Save the workbook
saveWorkbook(wb, 
             "/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/Humanized_mice_OD/GO_enrichment_by_cluster_OL.xlsx", 
             overwrite = TRUE)

cat("\n✓ GO enrichment results saved to Excel file!\n")

```

3.Plot the functional enrichment results results for GO terms


```{r,fig.height=20, fig.width=40}
# Collect GO BP results into one data.frame

ego_df <- map2_df(names(ego_list), ego_list, ~{
  if (is.null(.y) || nrow(as.data.frame(.y)) == 0) return(NULL)
  df <- as.data.frame(.y)
  df$cluster <- .x
  df$mLog10Padj <- -log10(df$p.adjust)
  df
})

# Choose top terms per cluster and plot

plot_df <- ego_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = mLog10Padj, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Description = fct_reorder(Description, mLog10Padj))

p1<-ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = cluster, y = Description, size = Count, color = mLog10Padj)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "gold", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "GO BP enrichment per cluster", x = "Cluster", y = "Term", color = "-log10(FDR)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) + theme_classic()+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 24,angle = 45),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 24),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size =28, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Arial", size =28, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 24,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 32,family = "Arial",face = "bold")
  ) 
plots <- list(Func_enrich_GO=p1)
for (nm in names(plots)) {
ggplot2::ggsave(file.path("/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/Plots/", paste0(nm, ".png")), plots[[nm]], width =14, height = 16, dpi = 300)
}
p1

```
##8.2 Functional enrichment analysis using KEGG pathways
Functional enrichment using Kegg pathways

1.Kegg pathways analysis

```{r}
ekegg_list <- lapply(names(entrez_by_cluster), function(cl){
  enrichKEGG(gene          = entrez_by_cluster[[cl]],
             organism      = "mmu",
             pvalueCutoff  = 0.05)
})
names(ekegg_list) <- names(entrez_by_cluster)


kegg_df <- map2_df(names(ekegg_list), ekegg_list, ~{
  if (is.null(.y) || nrow(as.data.frame(.y)) == 0) return(NULL)
  df <- as.data.frame(.y)
  df$cluster <- .x
  df$mLog10Padj <- -log10(df$p.adjust)
  df
})
```

2.Create an Excel file to save the results, to be attached as supplementary material

```{r}

# Save to Excel - Option 1: One sheet per cluster
wb_kegg <- createWorkbook()

for (cluster_name in names(ekegg_list)) {
  
  if (!is.null(ekegg_list[[cluster_name]]) && nrow(ekegg_list[[cluster_name]]@result) > 0) {
    
    # Get results as data frame
    kegg_results <- as.data.frame(ekegg_list[[cluster_name]])
    
    # Add mLog10Padj column
    kegg_results$mLog10Padj <- -log10(kegg_results$p.adjust)
    
    # Create sheet name
    sheet_name <- substr(cluster_name, 1, 31)
    
    # Add worksheet and write data
    addWorksheet(wb_kegg, sheet_name)
    writeData(wb_kegg, sheet_name, kegg_results)
    
    cat(sprintf("Added sheet for cluster: %s (%d KEGG pathways)\n", 
                cluster_name, nrow(kegg_results)))
    
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("No significant KEGG pathways for cluster: %s\n", cluster_name))
  }
}

# Save workbook with one sheet per cluster
saveWorkbook(wb_kegg, 
             "/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/Humanized_mice_OD/KEGG_enrichment_by_cluster_OL.xlsx", 
             overwrite = TRUE)

cat("\n✓ KEGG enrichment results saved (one sheet per cluster)!\n")

```

3.Plot the results

```{r,fig.height=20, fig.width=40}
plot_df_kegg <- kegg_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = mLog10Padj, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Description = fct_reorder(Description, mLog10Padj))

p1<-ggplot(plot_df_kegg, aes(x = cluster, y = Description, size = Count, color = mLog10Padj)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "gold", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "KEGG enrichment per cluster", x = "Cluster", y = "Pathway", color = "-log10(FDR)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)+theme_classic()+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 24,angle = 45),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 24),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size =28, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Arial", size =28, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 24,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 32,family = "Arial",face = "bold")
  ) 
plots <- list(Func_enrich_KEGG=p1)
for (nm in names(plots)) {
ggplot2::ggsave(file.path("/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/Plots/", paste0(nm, ".png")), plots[[nm]], width =14, height = 16, dpi = 300)
}
p1

```

1.Perform label transfer between the MOL dataset and the Pandey_dataset

```{r}
DefaultAssay(OL)<-"RNA"
OL[["RNA"]] <- JoinLayers(OL[["RNA"]])
```

```{r}
# Load and capture the original object name
loaded_name <- load("/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/oligos_final_pub.RObj") #Pandey Dataset can be donwloaded from Zenodo

# Assign it to DA_humanized_mouse_brain using the captured name
DA_humanized_mouse_brain <- get(loaded_name[1])

DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5 <- UpdateSeuratObject(object =DA_humanized_mouse_brain)
DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5@version

OL[["RNA"]] <- as(OL[["RNA"]], "Assay5")
OL[["dummy"]] <- CreateAssayObject(counts = GetAssayData(OL, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")[1:10, ])

transfer_anchors <- FindTransferAnchors(
  reference = DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5,
  query = OL,
  reference.assay = "RNA",
  query.assay = "RNA",
  reduction = "cca",
  npcs = 50,
  dims = 1:50,
  features = VariableFeatures(OL@assays[["RNA"]])
)

OL[["dummy"]] <- NULL
  

OL<- MapQuery(
  anchorset =transfer_anchors, 
  reference = DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5,
  query =  OL,
  refdata = "finalInterps")
#rename predictions columns 
cn <- colnames(OL@meta.data)
cn[cn == "predicted.id"] <- "predicted.id_DA_humanized_mouse_brain"
cn[cn == "predicted.id.score"] <- "predicted.id.score_DA_humanized_mouse_brain"
colnames(OL@meta.data) <- cn
```
2.Plot the UMAP with clusters renamed based on the label transfer results and the similarity score of each cluster of MOLs with the cluster from Pandey et al. both to be inserted in the main figure of the scRNA

```{r}
# Combine palettes to get 23 distinct colors

colors<-  c(
  "#0072B2", "#E69F00", "green", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7",
  "red", "#F0E442", "#000000", "#332288", "#88CCEE",
  "#44AA99", "#117733", "#999933", "#DDCC77", "#661100",
  "#AA4499", "#882255"
)

p1<-DimPlot(MOL ,reduction = "umap_MOL",group.by = "predicted.id_DA_humanized_mouse_brain",cols = colors)+
  labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2") +
  ggtitle("DA humanized_mouse_brains Clusters") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )


plots <- list(umap_MOL_predicted.id_DA_humanized_mouse_brain=p1)
for (nm in names(plots)) {
ggplot2::ggsave(file.path("/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/Plots/", paste0(nm, ".png")), plots[[nm]], width =12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
}
p1
rm(DA_humanized_mouse_brain)
rm(DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5)
gc()
```

3.Violin plot of each cluster similarity score. This code takes the prediction score associated to each cell in the MOL cluster and plot the predicted value 

```{r}
# Get cluster identities (your 5 populations)
clusters <- unique(MOL$MOL_fine_clustering)
print(paste("Creating plots for", length(clusters), "clusters"))

# Loop through each cluster
for (cluster_id in clusters) {
  
  # Create empty data frame to store all scores
  all_scores <- data.frame()
  
  # Get the prediction.score.id assay
  assay_data <- GetAssayData(MOL, assay = "prediction.score.id", slot = "data")
  
  # Get cells in this cluster
  cluster_cells <- colnames(MOL)[MOL$MOL_fine_clustering == cluster_id]
  
  # Extract scores for each of the 17 reference cell types
  for (ref_celltype in rownames(assay_data)) {
    scores <- assay_data[ref_celltype, cluster_cells]
    
    temp_df <- data.frame(
      score = as.numeric(scores),
      reference_celltype = ref_celltype,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    all_scores <- rbind(all_scores, temp_df)
  }
  
  # Create violin plot with all 17 reference cell types on x-axis
  p <- ggplot(all_scores, aes(x = reference_celltype, y = score, fill = reference_celltype)) +
    geom_violin(trim = TRUE, scale = "width") +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA) +
    ggtitle(paste("Cluster:", cluster_id)) +
    xlab("Reference Cell Type") +
    ylab("Similarity Score") +
    ylim(0, 1) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 36, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", 
                                 size = 14, angle = 90),  # Smaller for 17 labels
      axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 20),
      axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 24, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 24, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
  
   # Display plot in the R Markdown output
  print(p)
  
  # Save plot
  filename <- file.path("/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/Plots/", 
                       paste0("Similarity_cluster_DAO_humanized_mouse_brain", cluster_id, ".png"))
  ggplot2::ggsave(filename, p, width = 24, height = 12, dpi = 300)
  
  print(paste("✓ Saved plot for cluster:", cluster_id))
}

```

Feature plot MOL

```{r,fig.height=80, fig.width=60}
markers <- c("mm10-Klk8","mm10-Itga8", "mm10-Tlr3", "mm10-Trim34a", #MOL1/2 EAE
             "mm10-Plin4","mm10-Sult1a1","mm10-Zfand4", #MOL5/6–EAE-a
             "mm10-S100a10","mm10-Rnase4","mm10-Tnfrsf1a",#MOL5/6–EAE-b
             "mm10-Serpina3n","mm10-Nlrc5","mm10-Igtp","mm10-Fgfr2","mm10-B2m", #DA OL
             "mm10-Piezo2","mm10-Masp1","mm10-C4b","mm10-Klk8","mm10-Il33", #DAO_1
             "mm10-Psmb8","mm10-Stat1","mm10-H2-T23","mm10-Psmb9", #IFN_DAO
             "mm10-Cdkn1a","mm10-Ccnd1","mm10-Nav3","mm10-Tmsb10","mm10-Lrrfip1" #cycling_DAO
             )
#Code to plot them and save them 
for (gene in markers) {
  p <- FeaturePlot(
    OL, 
    features = gene, 
    reduction = "umap_OL",
    cols = c("lightgrey", "magenta")
  )+
  labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2") +
  ggtitle( paste0(gene)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size =48,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 24),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 28),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank())
 print(p)
  # Save each plot
  #filename <- file.path("/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/Plots/", paste0("Feature_plot_humanized_mouse_brain_", gene, ".png"))
  #ggplot2::ggsave(filename, p, width = 20, height = 12, dpi = 300)
}
#code to plot the markers here in the vignette

# 1) create a list of plots (one per gene)
plots <- FeaturePlot(
  OL,
  features = markers,
  reduction = "umap_OL",
  cols = c("lightgrey", "magenta"),
  combine = FALSE
)

# 2) apply consistent theme / title formatting to every plot
plots <- lapply(plots, function(p) {
  p +
    labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      aspect.ratio = 1,
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 64, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 50),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 50),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 50),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 52),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_blank()
    )
})

# 3) combine into a grid (e.g., 2 columns)
p <- wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 4)
p
```

##4.1 Select only MOLs from the whole dataset

With this line of code we will create a new Seurat object that will contain only MOLs that we will use for the subsequents analysis steps

```{r}


Microglia <- subset(humanized_mouse_brain, idents = c("Microglia"))            


```

##4.2 Reclustering of MOLs

1.Renormalize the datasets and recluster using only MOL cells   

```{r}
DefaultAssay(Microglia)<-"RNA"

# Remove the Microgliad SCT assay 
Microglia[["SCT"]] <- NULL

# Now run SCTransform renormalizing with only the Microglia_OPC cells 
Microglia <- SCTransform(Microglia, vst.flavor = "v2", verbose = FALSE)

# Continue with custom-named reductions
Microglia <- RunPCA(Microglia, verbose = T, reduction.name = "pca_Microglia_SCT")


Microglia<- FindNeighbors(Microglia, reduction = "pca_Microglia_SCT", dims = 1:15, 
                      graph.name = c("Microglia_nn", "Microglia_snn"))

Microglia <- FindClusters(Microglia, resolution = 0.1, 
                     graph.name = "Microglia_snn",
                     cluster.name = "Microglia_clusters")

Microglia <- RunUMAP(Microglia, reduction = "pca_Microglia_SCT", dims = 1:15, 
                reduction.name = "umap_Microglia")


```

```{r}
p1<-DimPlot(Microglia, reduction = "umap_Microglia", group.by = c("Microglia_clusters"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with new clustering
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p2<-DimPlot(Microglia, reduction = "umap_Microglia", group.by = c("orig.ident"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p3<-DimPlot(Microglia, reduction = "umap_Microglia", group.by = c("sample"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p4<-DimPlot(Microglia, reduction = "umap_Microglia", group.by = c("broad_clustering"))+labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2")+ #UMAP with samples
 theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )

p1
p2
p3
p4
```

Marker features calculations in OLs

```{r}

# find markers for every cluster compared to all remaining cells, report only the positive
# ones

DefaultAssay(Microglia)<-"SCT"
Microglia<- PrepSCTFindMarkers(Microglia, assay = "SCT",verbose = T)
Microglia_markers<- FindAllMarkers(Microglia, only.pos = TRUE)


 # Create a new workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# Loop through each cluster and add data to a new sheet
for (cluster_value in unique(Microglia_markers$cluster)) {
  cluster_data <- subset(Microglia_markers, cluster == cluster_value)
  sheet_name <- paste0("Cluster_", cluster_value)
  addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
  writeData(wb, sheet_name, cluster_data)
}

# Save the workbook to a file
saveWorkbook(wb, "/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/Humanized_mice_OD/Seurat_clusters_Microglia.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```

Plotting cluster Markers



```{r,fig.height=20, fig.width=30}



markers <- c(
  # Cluster 0 - Neuronal contamination (Cerebellar Granule Cells)
  "mm10-Kcnip4","mm10-Kcnd2","mm10-Fgf14","mm10-Ralyl","mm10-Nrxn1","mm10-Rims1","mm10-Rbfox1","mm10-Tenm1","mm10-Car10","mm10-Negr1",
  # Cluster 1 - Homeostatic Microglia
  "mm10-Plxdc2","mm10-Tanc2","mm10-Csmd3","mm10-Nav2","mm10-Srgap2","mm10-Zfhx3","mm10-Rapgef5","mm10-Tgfbr1","mm10-Elmo1","mm10-Frmd4a",
  # Cluster 2 - Neuronal contamination (GABAergic/Glutamatergic)
  "mm10-Lrrc7","mm10-Grin2b","mm10-Kcnh7","mm10-Tenm3","mm10-Grm5","mm10-Epha6","mm10-Meis2","mm10-Myo16","mm10-Sntg1","mm10-Cpne4",
  # Cluster 3 - Oligodendrocyte contamination
  "mm10-Prr5l","mm10-Sec14l5","mm10-Slc24a2","mm10-Edil3","mm10-Arap2","mm10-Efnb3","mm10-Mog","mm10-D7Ertd443e","mm10-Mag","mm10-Ppp1r16b",
  # Cluster 4 - Activated/Phagocytic Microglia (DAM-like)
  "mm10-Arhgap24","mm10-Fkbp5","mm10-Gcnt2","mm10-C1qa","mm10-Ssh2","mm10-Cd244a","mm10-C1qb","mm10-Man2b1","mm10-Cd300lf","mm10-Npl",
  # Cluster 5 - Microglia - metabolically active / stressed
  "mm10-Cst3","mm10-Psap","mm10-mt-Co3","mm10-mt-Atp6","mm10-mt-Co2","mm10-mt-Co1","mm10-Mef2a","mm10-Snx24","mm10-Inpp5d","mm10-Rps27a"
)
  


p1<-DotPlot(OL, features = markers,group.by = "OL_clusters",cols = c("lightgrey", "firebrick"))+
  xlab("")+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = markers) + 
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 24,angle = 90),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size =24),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24,family = "Arial")
  ) 
p1
```
1.Compute the GO terms significantly enriched

```{r}
# marker genes computed above

pos_markers <- Microglia_markers%>%
  dplyr::select(cluster, gene)

# convert symbMicroglias to ENTREZID per cluster
marker_list <- split(pos_markers$gene, pos_markers$cluster)

# convert once (union) and map back
all_genes <- unique(unlist(marker_list))

all_genes_clean <- sub("^mm10-", "", all_genes)

gene_map <- bitr(all_genes_clean, fromType = "SYMBOL", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db)
sym2ent <- setNames(gene_map$ENTREZID, gene_map$SYMBOL)

marker_list_clean <- lapply(marker_list, function(x) sub("^mm10-", "", x))

entrez_by_cluster <- lapply(marker_list_clean, function(x) na.omit(unname(sym2ent[x])))

ego_list <- lapply(names(entrez_by_cluster), function(cl){
  enrichGO(gene          = entrez_by_cluster[[cl]],
           OrgDb         = org.Mm.eg.db,
           keyType       = "ENTREZID",
           ont           = "BP",
           pAdjustMethod = "BH",
           pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
           qvalueCutoff  = 0.2,
           readable      = TRUE)
}) #list of enriched terms per cluster
names(ego_list) <- names(entrez_by_cluster)

```


2.Create an Excel file to save the results, to be attached as supplementary material

```{r}

# Create a new workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# Loop through each cluster and add enrichment results as a sheet
for (cluster_name in names(ego_list)) {
  
  # Extract results from enrichResult object
  if (!is.null(ego_list[[cluster_name]]) && nrow(ego_list[[cluster_name]]@result) > 0) {
    
    # Get the results as a data frame
    go_results <- as.data.frame(ego_list[[cluster_name]])
    
    # Create sheet name (Excel has 31 char limit for sheet names)
    sheet_name <- substr(cluster_name, 1, 31)
    
    # Add worksheet
    addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
    
    # Write data
    writeData(wb, sheet_name, go_results)
    
    cat(sprintf("Added sheet for cluster: %s (%d GO terms)\n", 
                cluster_name, nrow(go_results)))
    
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("No significant GO terms for cluster: %s\n", cluster_name))
  }
}

# Save the workbook
saveWorkbook(wb, 
             "/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/Humanized_mice_OD/GO_enrichment_by_cluster_Microglia.xlsx", 
             overwrite = TRUE)

cat("\n✓ GO enrichment results saved to Excel file!\n")

```


3.Plot the functional enrichment results results for GO terms


```{r,fig.height=20, fig.width=40}
# Collect GO BP results into one data.frame

ego_df <- map2_df(names(ego_list), ego_list, ~{
  if (is.null(.y) || nrow(as.data.frame(.y)) == 0) return(NULL)
  df <- as.data.frame(.y)
  df$cluster <- .x
  df$mLog10Padj <- -log10(df$p.adjust)
  df
})

# Choose top terms per cluster and plot

plot_df <- ego_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = mLog10Padj, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Description = fct_reorder(Description, mLog10Padj))

p1<-ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = cluster, y = Description, size = Count, color = mLog10Padj)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "gold", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "GO BP enrichment per cluster", x = "Cluster", y = "Term", color = "-log10(FDR)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) + theme_classic()+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text( hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", size = 24,angle = 45),
    axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 24),
    axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size =28, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Arial", size =28, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 24,family = "Arial"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 32,family = "Arial",face = "bold")
  ) 
plots <- list(Func_enrich_GO=p1)
for (nm in names(plots)) {
ggplot2::ggsave(file.path("/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/Plots/", paste0(nm, ".png")), plots[[nm]], width =14, height = 16, dpi = 300)
}
p1

```






7.Compute and save cluster markers of the different population in the MOL subclustering. This will be one of the supplementary tables to be attached to be included. 

```{r}

# find markers for every cluster compared to all remaining cells, report only the positive
# ones

DefaultAssay(MOL)<-"SCT"
MOL <- PrepSCTFindMarkers(MOL, assay = "SCT",verbose = T)
MOL_markers <- FindAllMarkers(MOL, only.pos = TRUE)


 # Create a new workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# Loop through each cluster and add data to a new sheet
for (cluster_value in unique(MOL_markers$cluster)) {
  cluster_data <- subset(MOL_markers, cluster == cluster_value)
  sheet_name <- paste0("Cluster_", cluster_value)
  addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
  writeData(wb, sheet_name, cluster_data)
}

# Save the workbook to a file
saveWorkbook(wb, "/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/Tables/Seurat_clusters_MOL_scRNA_10x.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
```

##5.Comparison with other datasets of DAOs and EAE
In this section the MOL dataset will be compared with other datasets in which DAOs have been described to define which type are induced by FGF. 

##5.1 Label transfer with panDAO paper Pandey et al., Cell Reports, 2022 
The first dataset that will be compared to our MOLs is a panDAO datasets cross studies which includes DAO from different type of disease model. 

1.Perform label transfer between the MOL dataset and the Pandey_dataset

```{r}
DefaultAssay(MOL)<-"RNA"
MOL[["RNA"]] <- JoinLayers(MOL[["RNA"]])
```

```{r}
# Load and capture the original object name
loaded_name <- load("/proj/user/mattia/humanized_mouse_brains_final_pub.RObj") #Pandey Dataset can be donwloaded from Zenodo

# Assign it to DA_humanized_mouse_brain using the captured name
DA_humanized_mouse_brain <- get(loaded_name[1])

DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5 <- UpdateSeuratObject(object =DA_humanized_mouse_brain)
DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5@version

transfer_anchors <- FindTransferAnchors(reference =DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5, query = MOL,
                                            reference.assay = "RNA",
                                        reduction = "cca",
                                        npcs = 50,
                                        dims = 1:50,
  query.assay = "RNA",
  features = VariableFeatures(MOL@assays[["RNA"]]),)
  

MOL<- MapQuery(
  anchorset =transfer_anchors, 
  reference = DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5,
  query =  MOL,
  refdata = "finalInterps")
#rename predictions columns 
cn <- colnames(MOL@meta.data)
cn[cn == "predicted.id"] <- "predicted.id_DA_humanized_mouse_brain"
cn[cn == "predicted.id.score"] <- "predicted.id.score_DA_humanized_mouse_brain"
colnames(MOL@meta.data) <- cn
```
2.Plot the UMAP with clusters renamed based on the label transfer results and the similarity score of each cluster of MOLs with the cluster from Pandey et al. both to be inserted in the main figure of the scRNA

```{r}
# Combine palettes to get 23 distinct colors

colors<-  c(
  "#0072B2", "#E69F00", "green", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7",
  "red", "#F0E442", "#000000", "#332288", "#88CCEE",
  "#44AA99", "#117733", "#999933", "#DDCC77", "#661100",
  "#AA4499", "#882255"
)

p1<-DimPlot(MOL ,reduction = "umap_MOL",group.by = "predicted.id_DA_humanized_mouse_brain",cols = colors)+
  labs(x = "UMAP 1", y = "UMAP 2") +
  ggtitle("DA humanized_mouse_brains Clusters") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size = 24,
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),         # Legend labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 24),  # Legend title
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank()
  )


plots <- list(umap_MOL_predicted.id_DA_humanized_mouse_brain=p1)
for (nm in names(plots)) {
ggplot2::ggsave(file.path("/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/Plots/", paste0(nm, ".png")), plots[[nm]], width =12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
}
p1
rm(DA_humanized_mouse_brain)
rm(DA_humanized_mouse_brain_v5)
gc()
```

3.Violin plot of each cluster similarity score. This code takes the prediction score associated to each cell in the MOL cluster and plot the predicted value 

```{r}
# Get cluster identities (your 5 populations)
clusters <- unique(MOL$MOL_fine_clustering)
print(paste("Creating plots for", length(clusters), "clusters"))

# Loop through each cluster
for (cluster_id in clusters) {
  
  # Create empty data frame to store all scores
  all_scores <- data.frame()
  
  # Get the prediction.score.id assay
  assay_data <- GetAssayData(MOL, assay = "prediction.score.id", slot = "data")
  
  # Get cells in this cluster
  cluster_cells <- colnames(MOL)[MOL$MOL_fine_clustering == cluster_id]
  
  # Extract scores for each of the 17 reference cell types
  for (ref_celltype in rownames(assay_data)) {
    scores <- assay_data[ref_celltype, cluster_cells]
    
    temp_df <- data.frame(
      score = as.numeric(scores),
      reference_celltype = ref_celltype,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    all_scores <- rbind(all_scores, temp_df)
  }
  
  # Create violin plot with all 17 reference cell types on x-axis
  p <- ggplot(all_scores, aes(x = reference_celltype, y = score, fill = reference_celltype)) +
    geom_violin(trim = TRUE, scale = "width") +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA) +
    ggtitle(paste("Cluster:", cluster_id)) +
    xlab("Reference Cell Type") +
    ylab("Similarity Score") +
    ylim(0, 1) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 36, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, family = "Arial", 
                                 size = 14, angle = 90),  # Smaller for 17 labels
      axis.text.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 20),
      axis.title.x = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 24, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 24, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
  
   # Display plot in the R Markdown output
  print(p)
  
  # Save plot
  filename <- file.path("/date/gcb/gcb_MZ/MOL_FGF_10x/Plots/", 
                       paste0("Similarity_cluster_DAO_humanized_mouse_brain", cluster_id, ".png"))
  ggplot2::ggsave(filename, p, width = 24, height = 12, dpi = 300)
  
  print(paste("✓ Saved plot for cluster:", cluster_id))
}

```

##5.2 Module score calculation using the same gene module used in Pandey et al.
In this section using the same gene set from Pandey et al., their enrichment into the MOL clusters will be performed. 

1.Extract the module from the supplementary material of Pandey et al., this code is structured in a way that uniforms the gene names since in the dataset from the paper some gene names are different than the ones used in the MOLs data

```{r}

# Read markers
DA_markers <- read.csv2("/proj/user/mattia/Markers_Cell_rep_2022.csv")

# Extract UPREGULATED genes for each signature
genes_DA1_up <- DA_markers %>% 
  dplyr::filter(DAO_fine_upregulatedGenes == "DA1") %>% 
  dplyr::select(symbol)

genes_DA2_up <- DA_markers %>% 
  dplyr::filter(DAO_fine_upregulatedGenes == "DA2") %>% 
  dplyr::select(symbol)

genes_IFN_up <- DA_markers %>% 
  dplyr::filter(DAO_fine_upregulatedGenes == "DA1_IFN") %>% 
  dplyr::select(symbol)

# Extract DOWNREGULATED genes for each signature
genes_DA1_down <- DA_markers %>% 
  dplyr::filter(DAO_fine_downregulatedGenes == "DA1") %>% 
  dplyr::select(symbol)

genes_DA2_down <- DA_markers %>% 
  dplyr::filter(DAO_fine_downregulatedGenes == "DA2") %>% 
  dplyr::select(symbol)

genes_IFN_down <- DA_markers %>% 
  dplyr::filter(DAO_fine_downregulatedGenes == "DA1_IFN") %>% 
  dplyr::select(symbol)

# Create lists with both up and down genes
sets <- list(
  DA1 = list(up = genes_DA1_up$symbol, down = genes_DA1_down$symbol),
  DA2 = list(up = genes_DA2_up$symbol, down = genes_DA2_down$symbol),
  IFN = list(up = genes_IFN_up$symbol, down = genes_IFN_down$symbol)
)

# Replacement dictionary for gene name corrections
repl <- c(
  "Cac1h"   = "Cacna1h",
  "Kcb1"    = "Kcnb1",
  "Plx1"    = "Plxna1",
  "Serpi3n" = "Serpina3n",
  "Rse4"    = "Rgs4",
  "prt"     = "Prt",
  "Sp91"    = "Spp1",
  "v3"      = "Vcan"
)

# Function to apply replacements to a character vector
apply_replacements <- function(x, map) {
  i <- match(x, names(map), nomatch = 0L)
  x[which(i > 0L)] <- map[i[i > 0L]]
  unique(x)
}

# Apply replacements to NESTED lists (up and down separately)
sets_fixed <- lapply(sets, function(gene_set) {
  list(
    up = apply_replacements(gene_set$up, repl),
    down = apply_replacements(gene_set$down, repl)
  )
})

# Filter to keep only genes present in your Seurat object
present_only <- function(v, obj) intersect(v, rownames(obj))

sets_fixed_present <- lapply(sets_fixed, function(gene_set) {
  list(
    up = present_only(gene_set$up, MOL),
    down = present_only(gene_set$down, MOL)
  )
})

# Inspect what was changed - FIXED VERSION
changed <- lapply(names(sets), function(nm) {
  # Process up-regulated genes
  old_up <- sets[[nm]]$up
  new_up <- sets_fixed[[nm]]$up
  changed_up <- old_up[old_up %in% names(repl)]
  
  # Process down-regulated genes
  old_down <- sets[[nm]]$down
  new_down <- sets_fixed[[nm]]$down
  changed_down <- old_down[old_down %in% names(repl)]
  
  # Combine both
  df_up <- if(length(changed_up) > 0) {
    data.frame(
      set = nm,
      direction = "up",
      from = changed_up,
      to = repl[changed_up],
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else NULL
  
  df_down <- if(length(changed_down) > 0) {
    data.frame(
      set = nm,
      direction = "down",
      from = changed_down,
      to = repl[changed_down],
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else NULL
  
  rbind(df_up, df_down)
})

changed <- do.call(rbind, changed)
print("Gene name changes:")
print(changed)

# Check missing genes
missing_in_obj <- lapply(names(sets_fixed), function(nm) {
  list(
    up = setdiff(sets_fixed[[nm]]$up, rownames(MOL)),
    down = setdiff(sets_fixed[[nm]]$down, rownames(MOL))
  )
})
names(missing_in_obj) <- names(sets_fixed)

print("\nGenes missing in object:")
print(missing_in_obj)

```


2. To calculate the gene set score the same way as in Pandey et al. an ad hoc function need to be created, to apply the same methodology to perform the calculation. 


3.Apply the funciton and calculate the score
```{r}
DefaultAssay(MOL) <- "RNA"
MOL[["RNA"]] <- JoinLayers(MOL[["RNA"]])
# Calculate signed scores for each gene set
cat("Calculating DA1 signed score:\n")
MOL$DA1_score <- calculate_signed_score(
  MOL, 
  geneset_up = sets$DA1$up,
  geneset_down = sets$DA1$down,
  assay = "RNA"
)

cat("\nCalculating DA2 signed score:\n")
MOL$DA2_score <- calculate_signed_score(
  MOL, 
  geneset_up = sets$DA2$up,
  geneset_down = sets$DA2$down,
  assay = "RNA"
)

cat("\nCalculating IFN signed score:\n")
MOL$IFN_score <- calculate_signed_score(
  MOL, 
  geneset_up = sets$IFN$up,
  geneset_down = sets$IFN$down,
  assay = "RNA"
)


```

4.Plot the score that should go in figure 3. In this code the enrichment for each set is plotted separately


```{r}

# Select the grouping variable used in the plot (e.g., cluster_renamed)
grp <- "MOL_fine_clustering"  # or "seurat_clusters" or your custom label

# Panel C: DA1-enriched gene set score across OL subtypes
pC <- ggplot(MOL@meta.data, aes_string(x = grp, y = "DA1_score", color = grp)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 0.7, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_classic(base_family = "Arial") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 24),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "MOL5/6_DA1 enriched gene set score")

# Panel D: DA2-enriched
pD <- ggplot(MOL@meta.data, aes_string(x = grp, y = "DA2_score", color = grp)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 0.7, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_classic(base_family = "Arial") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 24),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 28, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "MOL5/6_DA2 enriched gene set score")

# Panel E: IFN-enriched
pE <- ggplot(MOL@meta.data, aes_string(x = grp, y = "IFN_score", color = grp)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 0.7, alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_classic(base_family = "Arial") +
```
